bibliographic_data.title: SCALAR: A Part-of-speech Tagger for Identifiers
bibliographic_data.abstract: The paper presents the Source Code Analysis and Lexical Annotation Runtime (SCALAR), a tool specialized for mapping source code identifier names to their corresponding part-of-speech tag sequence (grammar pattern). SCALAR’s internal model is trained using scikit-learn’s GradientBoostingClassifier in conjunction with a manually-curated oracle of identifier names and their grammar patterns. This specializes the tagger to recognize the unique structure of the natural language used by developers to create all types of identifiers (e.g., function names, variable names etc.). SCALAR’s output is compared with a previous version of the tagger, as well as a modern off-the-shelf part-of-speech tagger to show how it improves upon other taggers’ output for annotating identifiers.

core_content.novelty_claim: SCALAR is a specialized part-of-speech tagging approach designed for source code identifiers, which uses scikit-learn’s GradientBoostingClassifier to train on manually-curated data and generate grammar patterns.
core_content.key_methodology: SCALAR uses scikit-learn’s GradientBoostingClassifier in conjunction with a manually-curated oracle of identifier names and their grammar patterns. It is designed to recognize the unique structure of natural language used by developers for identifiers.
outcomes: Improved accuracy compared to previous taggers
Generates grammar pattern sequences useful for future research
core_content.full_text_markdown: The paper introduces SCALAR, a tool that maps source code identifier names to their corresponding part-of-speech (PoS) tags. The internal model of SCALAR is trained using scikit-learn’s GradientBoostingClassifier with manually-curated data. This approach specializes the tagger to recognize the unique structure used by developers in creating identifiers such as function and variable names. The tool's output is compared against previous versions and modern off-the-shelf PoS taggers, demonstrating its effectiveness. SCALAR is intended for use in future research on identifier naming practices and improving developer reading efficiency.
